skill.name: hugging-face-jobs
skill.description: Run Python workloads on Hugging Face cloud infrastructure using UV scripts or Docker containers with GPU/TPU support.
skill.summary: Execute ML workloads, data processing, and batch inference on managed Hugging Face Jobs infrastructure.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: All 284 static analysis findings are false positives. The 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' detections are actually Python code examples in markdown documentation. 'Hardcoded URLs' are legitimate documentation links to Hugging Face resources. Environment variable access (HF_TOKEN) is documented authentication behavior for Hub operations. No malicious patterns detected.

content.user_title: Execute ML workloads on Hugging Face cloud
content.value_statement: Run GPU/TPU workloads without local hardware setup. Submit Python scripts to managed Hugging Face Jobs infrastructure with secure token authentication.
actual_capabilities: Submit UV scripts with inline Python code to cloud GPUs/TPUs
Run Docker-based jobs with custom images and commands
Authenticate securely with HF_TOKEN for Hub operations
Schedule recurring jobs with CRON expressions
Monitor job status, view logs, and cancel running jobs
Persist results to Hugging Face Hub datasets and models
limitations: Requires paid Hugging Face Pro, Team, or Enterprise plan
Jobs run asynchronously - results must be persisted to Hub or external storage
Local file paths do not work with hf_jobs() MCP tool - scripts must be inline code or URLs
Default 30-minute timeout may be insufficient for long-running training jobs

use_cases.title: ML Engineer running batch inference
use_cases.description: Process thousands of samples through a model using cloud GPUs without local hardware investment. Submit UV scripts with vLLM for high-throughput generation.

use_cases.title: Data Scientist transforming datasets
use_cases.description: Execute data processing pipelines on Hugging Face datasets using Polars or Pandas. Push transformed results back to Hub securely.

use_cases.title: Researcher running experiments
use_cases.description: Execute reproducible ML experiments with scheduled jobs. Monitor progress via job URLs and retrieve results from Hub repositories.

prompt_templates.title: Basic script execution
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this Python script on Hugging Face Jobs: [paste script]. Use CPU and 30 minute timeout.

prompt_templates.title: GPU inference with token authentication
prompt_templates.prompt: Execute this inference script on an A10G GPU. Push results to my Hub repo username/results. Include HF_TOKEN authentication.

prompt_templates.title: Scheduled data processing
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a scheduled job that runs this data transformation script daily at 9 AM. Use cpu-upgrade hardware and persist output to Hub.

prompt_templates.title: Docker-based custom workload
prompt_templates.prompt: Run this command using the pytorch/pytorch CUDA image on an A100 GPU. Set timeout to 4 hours and monitor for completion.

output_examples.input: Submit UV script for sentiment analysis
output_examples.output: Job submitted successfully. Job ID: job_abc123. Monitor at https://huggingface.co/jobs/username/job_abc123. Estimated completion: 5 minutes.
Found 2 running jobs: job_abc123 (RUNNING, 45% complete), job_def456 (QUEUED). Use hf_jobs('logs', {'job_id': 'job_abc123'}) to view logs.

best_practices: Always use secrets={'HF_TOKEN': '$HF_TOKEN'} for Hub authentication - never hardcode tokens
Set appropriate timeouts with 20-30% buffer for your workload type
Persist results to Hub or external storage before job completion - environment is ephemeral
anti_patterns: Using local file paths with hf_jobs() MCP tool - scripts must be inline code or URLs
Polling job status repeatedly - wait for user to request status checks
Using env instead of secrets for tokens - env variables are visible in logs

faq.question: Do I need a paid Hugging Face plan to use Jobs?
faq.answer: Yes. Hugging Face Jobs requires a Pro, Team, or Enterprise plan. Free accounts cannot submit jobs.

faq.question: How do I authenticate my jobs with the Hub?
faq.answer: Use secrets={'HF_TOKEN': '$HF_TOKEN'} in your job config. The $HF_TOKEN placeholder is automatically replaced with your logged-in token.

faq.question: What happens to my results after the job finishes?
faq.answer: All files are deleted when the job ends. You must push results to Hugging Face Hub, upload to cloud storage (S3/GCS), or send via API before completion.

faq.question: Can I use local script files with hf_jobs()?
faq.answer: No. The hf_jobs() MCP tool requires inline script content or a URL. Read local files with Path('script.py').read_text() and pass the content string.

faq.question: How do I choose the right GPU for my workload?
faq.answer: Start with t4-small for testing. Use a10g-large for 7-13B models. Use a100-large for 13B+ models. See the hardware guide in this skill for detailed recommendations.

faq.question: Can I schedule jobs to run automatically?
faq.answer: Yes. Use hf_jobs('scheduled uv', {...}) with a schedule parameter like '@daily' or CRON expression '0 9 * * 1' for 9 AM Mondays.
